top_signals.title: Gold Extends Gains as Trump Touts Reopening of Hormuz This Week
top_signals.summary: Gold climbed further after US President Donald Trump said the Strait of Hormuz could reopen on Friday, easing an energy and inflation shock that has roiled global markets.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: German Investor Outlook Jumps With Iran Resolution in Sight
top_signals.summary: German investor optimism improved more than anticipated on hopes that a swift end to the Iran war will ease pressure on Europe’s largest economy.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Carbon emissions of ECB and Eurosystem portfolios continue to decline
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: CFTC chair Selig defends decision to approve ‘perps’ in U.S.
top_signals.summary: Michael Selig said that incumbents will always fear the future, but it's important to develop the new asset class domestically rather than only offshore.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: China's nuclear power capacity nearly doubled since 2016
top_signals.summary: From 2016 to 2024, China's nuclear generation capacity increased 76% (24 GW), based on our International Energy Statistics (IES) data. According to the International Atomic Energy Agency's Power Reactor Information System (PRIS), China added an additional 1.1 GW of nuclear power capacity in 2025 and 2.2 GW in 2026 (through May). China is continuing to build out its nuclear generating capacity and has 36 reactors under construction, accounting for more than 49% of total world nuclear construction, according to PRIS.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: India's solution to entrance exam fraud: A temporary ban on Telegram
top_signals.summary: India has temporarily restricted access to the messaging app Telegram in an effort to prevent exam fraud.

top_signals.title: U.S. natural gas storage capacity increased slightly in 2025
top_signals.summary: Underground working natural gas storage capacity in the Lower 48 states increased slightly in 2025, according to our latest data, with growth concentrated in the South Central and Mountain regions. Underground natural gas storage provides a source of energy when demand increases, balancing U.S. energy needs. We calculate natural gas storage capacity in two ways: demonstrated peak capacity and working gas design capacity. Both increased in 2025.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
